name: Via Verde del Val de Zafán – circular from La Fresneda
name_original: Via Verde del Val de Zafán – circular desde La Fresneda
region_intro: Touring cycling around La Fresneda offers diverse landscapes within the Matarraña region of Aragon, Spain. The area features a network of back roads and paths, including the Vía Verde del Val del Zafán, a restored railway track. Cyclists can traverse olive groves, almond trees, and vineyards, with routes ranging from flat greenways to more challenging terrain with elevation changes. The region also includes natural features like rivers and impressive rock formations. Best touring cycling routes around La Fresneda The most popular touring cycling route is Via Verde del Val de Zafán – circular from La Fresneda, a 17.3 miles (27.8 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route follows a section of the historic Vía Verde del Val del Zafán. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Fresneda Main Square – Access to Pla Sensal loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda, a difficult 27.1 miles (43.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Valderrobres Stone Bridge – View of La Fresneda loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda, a 17.0 miles (27.4 km) trail leading through the Matarraña landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around La Fresneda is defined by the Vía Verde del Val del Zafán, extensive olive groves, and historic medieval villages. The network offers options for easy family-friendly routes as well as more challenging paths. The routes in La Fresneda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore La Fresneda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around La Fresneda?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around La Fresneda, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging paths to explore the beautiful Matarraña region.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in La Fresneda?
A: Yes, La Fresneda offers easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40347875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fresneda Main Square – La Fresneda loop from La Fresneda&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 8.3 km path with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed ride. The Vía Verde del Val del Zafán also provides a safe and scenic route, as motor vehicles are prohibited, making it ideal for all ages.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in La Fresneda?
A: The terrain around La Fresneda is quite varied. You can expect to cycle through extensive olive groves, almond trees, and vineyards. Some routes follow the flat, restored railway track of the Vía Verde del Val del Zafán, while others, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fresneda Main Square – Access to Pla Sensal loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda&lt;/a&gt;, feature significant elevation changes and more challenging sections.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of the diverse landscapes of the Matarraña region. You might encounter impressive rock formations like the Rocks of Masmut, or natural water features such as the Peña Reservoir, where you can enjoy kayaking. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable natural monument in the area, particularly spectacular after rainfall.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955927439/via-verde-del-val-de-zafan-circular-from-la-fresneda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde del Val de Zafán – circular from La Fresneda&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 27.8 km ride. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31484259" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valderrobres Stone Bridge – View of La Fresneda loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.4 km trail through the landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in La Fresneda?
A: The Matarraña region is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Spring offers the added beauty of blooming landscapes and excellent bird watching opportunities. Summer can be warm, but the lack of light pollution makes it ideal for observing starry skies in the evenings.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in La Fresneda?
A: The komoot community highly rates the touring cycling experience in La Fresneda, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 25 reviews. Cyclists often praise the tranquil landscapes, the well-maintained Vía Verde del Val del Zafán, and the charming medieval villages that offer cultural interest along the routes.

Q: Can I find bike rentals and support services in La Fresneda?
A: Yes, for visitors, mountain bikes, e-bikes, and city bikes, along with helmets, are available for hire in the area. You can also typically find maps and detailed route descriptions to help plan your rides.

Q: Are there any historic sites or villages to explore along the routes?
A: Absolutely. La Fresneda itself is a historic-artistic monument with charming steep streets and medieval alleys. You can explore sites like the remains of the Arab-origin Castle of Santa Barbara, the Santa María la Mayor Parish Church, and the 16th-century Town Hall. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valderrobres Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is another notable historical landmark you might encounter on your rides.

Q: Is public transport available to access the cycling routes?
A: While specific public transport details for route access are not provided, the region is known for its network of back roads and paths. For longer distances, consider checking local bus services that connect towns within the Matarraña region, though direct access to trailheads might require personal transport.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the region is known for its beautiful landscapes and offers excellent panoramic views from various viewpoints. Cycling routes often traverse elevated areas, providing opportunities to see the expansive olive groves, vineyards, and the picturesque Matarraña scenery. The area around the Castle of Santa Barbara in La Fresneda offers great vantage points.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fresneda Main Square – Access to Pla Sensal loop from La Fresneda / la Freixneda&lt;/a&gt; are classified as difficult. This 43.6 km path features significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced touring cyclists looking for a demanding ride.
